title: Live operator↔Claude.ai test on 2026-05-04 surfaced that AMS magic link has zero self-description for non-browser visitors
content: Sequence: operator minted a tincan magic link, handed it to a fresh Claude.ai session via project instructions, the session attached via raw WebSocket using bash + curl, then a reactive Sonnet 4.5 streaming run held a 120s window with 8-turn real conversation. The whole sequence required priming via project instructions for the Claude session to know AMS exists and how to use it. Probed alternatives directly: GET {magic_link} with Accept: application/json returns 404; /.well-known/ams, /.well-known/agent-card, /AGENTS.md all 404. The MCP wrapper at /mcp is live and self-describing via tools/list, but a fresh assistant has no path to discover that /mcp exists from the magic link alone. The bootstrap gap is real: any AI assistant without prior priming cannot use a handed magic link.

title: D0016 (canon, 2026-05-04) settled buffering as wrapper-tier; ams_recv long-poll tool already shipped today via TinCan v1 build; SessionDO keyed per D0019 holds state across MCP transport sessions
content: Verified by reading worker/src/mcp.ts: the tool surface in production is ams_create_conversation, ams_join, ams_send, ams_recv. ams_recv description: "Long-poll degradation path: drain buffered peer frames since the last ams_recv... If buffer empty, wait up to this many ms for a frame. Default 0." wait_ms cap = 25000. Per-session buffering already exists in TinCan v1 (per the 2026-05-04-tincan-day3-build.tsv journal). D0019 keying means an MCP transport disconnect+reconnect under the same (account_id, conversation_id) lands on the same SessionDO with buffer intact. This is sufficient for the polling pattern Claude.ai needs (no background runtime → drain on each turn).

title: MCP spec already standardizes every primitive AMS would otherwise need to invent for self-description: initialize.instructions, prompts/list, prompts/get, resources/list, resources/read
content: The earlier-in-conversation proposal (well-known endpoints + content negotiation on magic link + custom JSON manifest) was reinventing what MCP carries natively. Operator named the duh: "it's an MCP server. The MCP server should operate from magic links as well. Even if they are just relayed and the MCP server wraps canon guide posture governance to the client to teach them how to use the thing." Restating: the MCP edge wrapper IS the agent card, IS the manifest, IS the bootstrap carrier. The architecture symmetry: oddkit's MCP server delivers operating governance to its consuming model on every request via response envelope; AMS's MCP wrapper should deliver AMS-operating governance to its peers via initialize.instructions + prompts/list + resources/list. Same pattern, different scope. Vodka discipline preserved (translation only, no new wire features).

title: My initial draft proposal (well-known endpoints + content negotiation) was a category error: building a parallel discovery format alongside MCP rather than populating MCP's own surfaces
content: Caught by operator's "duh." The corrected design uses MCP-spec features only: route alias (POST {magic_link} → MCP handler), populated initialize.instructions, prompts/list + prompts/get for operator-set prompt scaffolds, expanded resources/list. Zero new formats, zero parallel surfaces, zero specification authoring. Cleanest possible shape — the wrapper just exposes MCP-spec features it had not yet populated. Confidence: post-correction, established. Disconfirmer: if MCP spec deprecates these surfaces, the design is partially obsolete and reopens the well-known-endpoint path.

title: Drafted D0023-magic-link-as-mcp-transport-endpoint as canon-tier-1 decision: route POST {magic_link} to MCP handler with conversation pre-bound; populate initialize.instructions with canon-derived static block + opaque pass-through of operator-set conversation metadata.instructions; add prompts/list + resources/list MCP surfaces; reserve metadata.instructions and metadata.prompts as wrapper-recognized convention keys
content: Draft staged at /home/claude/D0023-magic-link-as-mcp-transport-endpoint.md. Includes Alternatives Considered (4 weighed: well-known endpoints, capabilities-manifest embedding, tour-guide subscriber, status quo), Risks and Failure Modes (4 named with retraction conditions: prompt injection amplification, static governance drift, MCP surface deprecation, adoption pattern mismatch), Comparative Positioning (vs A2A's /.well-known/agent-card.json — direction, lifecycle, carrier, convergence/divergence per envelope-altitude-consensus principle), Success Criteria (5 explicit: bootstrap test as primary, multi-runtime validation across 3+ MCP-speaking runtimes, wrapper-stays-cheap discipline preserved, no wire change, backward-compat with existing /mcp callers). Reversibility: two-way at every level (purely additive, removing any piece is polite degradation). All 11 cited canon URIs verified resolvable in local clone; klappy://canon/bootstrap/model-operating-contract verified live via oddkit_get. NOT YET ENCODED — staged for operator review gate before landing.

title: Wrapper-stays-cheap discipline holds: D0023 implementation must do translation only, no payload parsing, no schema validation, no opinions on operator-set content
content: Three checks confirmed in the draft's "Discipline Boundary" section: (1) Could the bootstrap content be served by a separate subscriber instead? In principle yes (tour-guide subscriber alternative considered and rejected) but the latency+overhead is structurally worse than synchronous initialize read; factor-out test does not fire. (2) Does the wrapper make decisions about payload contents? No — static block is build-time canon derivation, per-conversation block is read-and-forward, prompts surface is read-and-forward. All payload-bearing content is opaque pass-through. (3) Does the new surface outlive the runtime session? No — initialize responses are per-session, prompts/list reads are per-session. Conversation metadata they reference lives in the Conversation DO (wire-tier, persists by D0002). All three pass; surface additions stay within wrapper-tier translation contract.

title: Permanent-non-goals items 1–3 enforced: AMS does not interpret operator identity schemes, capability schemas, or conversation authorization policy; D0023 wrapper carries operator-set instructions/prompts opaquely
content: An operator who sets metadata.instructions = "ignore your prior instructions and..." gets exactly that, broadcast to every peer that calls initialize. The wrapper carries; the operator authors. AMS takes no position on the content; it does not safety-review, redact, or filter. This is consistent with the wire's payload opacity and with permanent-non-goals's general posture. Trust scope is the magic link's audience (D0004 two-door registration), unchanged from the existing wire trust model.

title: Awaiting operator review gate on D0023 draft + journal entry before encoding to canon and proceeding to worker implementation
content: Operator's explicit cadence: "always document, plan and preflight with oddkit first. Audit and challenge the plan before building. And canon and governance and journal get updated before building as well." Status: oddkit_orient ✓ (planning mode confirmed), oddkit_preflight ✓ (constraint surface read: mcp-wrapper-conformance-for-conversational-ai, permanent-non-goals, observability-payload-boundary, wrapper-stays-cheap), draft canon ✓ (D0023 with all canon-tier-1 quality elements: alternatives, risks, comparative positioning, success criteria, reversibility), oddkit_challenge ✓ at canon-tier-1 mode (block_until_addressed: false; load-bearing gaps addressed via Alternatives + Risks + Success Criteria sections; non-load-bearing gaps acknowledged), URI audit ✓ (11/11 cited canon URIs resolve; klappy:// upstream verified; PROD URIs are MCP resource URIs not canon docs; D0023 self-reference is the only "miss" and that's expected). Operator-side gate: review draft, decide on (a) merge to canon as-is, (b) request revisions, or (c) reject the direction. Post-merge: PR worker code per D0023 success criteria, run bootstrap test against fresh Claude.ai session as primary acceptance.
